pi_items.source: Code analysis of setup_task.sh (line 9) and export_result.sh (line 86)
Web search confirms cnn.com is CNN's major news portal (https://www.cnn.com/). Code analysis confirms cnn.com in setup_task.sh line 54 and export_result.sh line 62.
Web search confirms weather.com is The Weather Channel's website (weather.com). Code analysis confirms weather.com in setup_task.sh line 55 and export_result.sh line 63.
Web search confirms wikipedia.org is Wikipedia's reference encyclopedia (https://www.wikipedia.org/). Code analysis confirms wikipedia.org in setup_task.sh line 56 and export_result.sh line 64.

pi_items.verified_value: All 12 tracker domains are legitimate, known advertising/tracking platforms: doubleclick (Google Ads), google-analytics (Google Analytics), googletagmanager (Google Tag Manager), facebook (Meta's tracking pixel), scorecardresearch (Comscore), amazon-adsystem (Amazon ads), adsrvr (The Trade Desk), criteo (retargeting platform), rubiconproject (Magnite/programmatic RTB), pubmatic (programmatic platform), outbrain (content recommendation/tracking), taboola (content recommendation/tracking)
pi_items.source: Web searches verified each tracker: doubleclick as Google's tracking domain, google-analytics as official Google Analytics service, googletagmanager as Google's tag management, facebook as Meta tracking, scorecardresearch confirmed as Comscore tracking vendor, amazon-adsystem as Amazon's ad system, adsrvr as The Trade Desk domain, criteo as major retargeting/tracking platform, rubiconproject as RTB platform (now Magnite), pubmatic as programmatic advertising, outbrain and taboola as content recommendation networks with tracking capabilities

privileged_info_summary: The task involves forensic analysis of real cookies from three major websites: CNN.com (major news portal), Weather.com (The Weather Channel), and Wikipedia.org (free encyclopedia). The task clears browser state and creates a briefing document at /home/ga/Desktop/forensic_briefing.txt, then expects an agent to visit these sites and analyze the cookies deposited. The agent must identify third-party tracking domains from a known list of 12 legitimate trackers: DoubleClick (Google), Google Analytics, Google Tag Manager, Facebook, Scorecardresearch (Comscore), Amazon-Adsystem, Adsrvr (The Trade Desk), Criteo, Rubicon Project, PubMatic, Outbrain, and Taboola. The resulting forensic report must be written to /home/ga/Desktop/cookie_forensic_report.txt and include cookie counts per site, specific tracking domain names, and comparative analysis of tracking intensity across sites.
